clinical_coherence.notes: 64F South Asian with bilateral parkinsonism on metoclopramide for gastroparesis. Similar confound to PD-M01. Premotor features mentioned. However, this case is extremely similar in structure to PD-M01 - same metoclopramide confound, same DaTscan differentiation, same RBD finding. The clinical presentation is well-constructed internally.

tool_output_consistency.notes: DaTscan abnormal bilateral. Levodopa challenge positive at 35.7%. Autonomic testing normal. PSG confirms RBD at 68% RWA. Drug interaction check appropriately flags metoclopramide. Minor issue: levodopa challenge interpretation says 'moderate rather than robust' response may be due to concurrent metoclopramide - good clinical reasoning.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G20 correct. Differential and critical actions well-structured. Correctly identifies need to discontinue metoclopramide before reliable PD assessment.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Nearly identical complexity to PD-M01 - same metoclopramide confound. While individually appropriate as moderate, having two virtually identical moderate cases reduces discriminative value of the benchmark.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 64F South Asian with DM2, HTN, hyperlipidemia, gastroparesis. Realistic profile. Age appropriate for PD.

educational_value.notes: Individually solid, but near-duplicate of PD-M01. Both cases teach the same lesson (metoclopramide vs idiopathic PD, DaTscan differentiation, RBD as biomarker). Limited incremental educational value beyond M01.

issues_found: Near-duplicate of PD-M01 in structure, confound, and teaching points
Lab interpretation mentions HbA1c 6.8% but actual value is not visible in reviewed sections
